doc.id: Potkay et al. - 2021 - Coupled whole-tree optimality and xylem hydraulics explain dynamic biomass partitioning.pdf
doc.title: Coupled whole-tree optimality and xylem hydraulics explain dynamic biomass partitioning
doc.source_file: /mnt/data/Potkay et al. - 2021 - Coupled whole-tree optimality and xylem hydraulics explain dynamic biomass partitioning.pdf

model_card.name: Potkay2021_S9_CarbonPoolDynamics_NSC_Growth
task: Represent uncoupled photosynthesis–growth dynamics via a nonstructural carbohydrate (NSC) storage pool
Update NSC pool using gross canopy assimilation, maintenance respiration, and phloem loading for growth plus construction respiration
Compute growth rate G as a linear function of NSC pool size modulated by air-temperature-dependent growth modifier gT(Ta)
Update structural carbon pools (leaf, sapwood, layered roots) using allocation fractions and senescence fluxes; route senesced sapwood to heartwood
Update basal diameter from wood growth and allometric derivative dD/dCW; update canopy geometry (H, W), leaf area, and downstream hydraulic capacities

assumptions.text: Growth and photosynthesis are uncoupled; growth is driven by an NSC storage pool that enables recovery after disturbances.
NSC is defined as starch NSC (not soluble sugars required for osmoregulation).
Growth dependence on NSC is simplified to the linear portion of a Michaelis–Menten relationship, so phloem loading for growth scales linearly with NSC pool size via a constant proportionality ζ.
A unitless temperature modifier gT(Ta) is applied to growth (Scots Pine parameterization), with gT(Ta)=0 for Ta<0°C and logistic form for Ta≥0°C.

equations.label: S.9.1
equations.explanation: NSC pool balance: filled by gross canopy carbon assimilation aL(An+Rd), depleted by maintenance respiration Rm and phloem loading for growth plus construction respiration at rate G/(1−fc).

equations.label: S.9.2a
equations.explanation: Total growth rate is proportional to NSC pool size (linearized), reduced by construction-respiration fraction, and modulated by temperature-dependent growth modifier gT.

equations.label: S.9.2b
equations.explanation: Piecewise temperature modifier for growth used in THORP (Scots Pine).

equations.label: S.9.3
equations.explanation: Leaf carbon pool dynamics: gains from allocation fraction uL of growth, losses from leaf senescence SL.

equations.label: S.9.4
equations.explanation: Sapwood carbon pool dynamics: gains from allocation fraction usW of growth, losses from sapwood senescence SsW.

equations.label: S.9.5
equations.explanation: Horizontal-root carbon pool in soil layer i: gains from allocation fraction uR,H,i, losses from senescence SR,H,i.

equations.label: S.9.6
equations.explanation: Vertical-root carbon pool in soil layer i: gains from allocation fraction uR,V,i, losses from senescence SR,V,i.

equations.label: S.9.7
equations.explanation: Senesced sapwood carbon is added to the heartwood pool at the sapwood senescence rate.

equations.label: S.9.8
equations.explanation: Basal diameter growth rate is driven by wood allocation usW·G scaled by the allometric derivative ∂D/∂CW (defined elsewhere).

evidence.pointer: Eq. (S.9.8) and the sentence referencing ∂D/∂CW definition

variables.name: NSC (storage) carbon pool
variables.definition: Nonstructural carbohydrate storage pool used to decouple photosynthesis and growth.

variables.name: leaf area
variables.definition: Leaf area scaling canopy carbon assimilation input into the NSC pool.

evidence.pointer: S.9 paragraph defining gross canopy assimilation aL·(An+Rd)

variables.name: net assimilation rate
variables.definition: Net leaf assimilation rate (from Section S.6), used in NSC filling term aL(An+Rd).

variables.name: leaf dark respiration rate
variables.definition: Leaf dark respiration rate, added to An to obtain gross canopy carbon assimilation.

variables.name: whole-tree maintenance respiration
variables.definition: Total maintenance respiration draining the NSC pool (defined in Section S.7).

variables.name: total growth rate
variables.definition: Growth rate allocated among structural pools; coupled to NSC pool size by Eq. (S.9.2a).

variables.name: construction respiration fraction
variables.definition: Fraction of carbon spent on construction respiration; phloem loading for growth+construction is G/(1−fc).

variables.name: growth proportionality constant
variables.definition: Proportionality constant linking NSC pool size to phloem loading (and thus growth) under the linearized assumption.

variables.name: temperature growth modifier
variables.definition: Unitless modifier describing growth temperature dependence as a function of air temperature Ta.

variables.name: air temperature
variables.definition: Air temperature driving gT(Ta) in the growth model.

variables.name: leaf allocation fraction
variables.definition: Fraction of total growth G allocated to leaves (defined in Section S.8).

variables.name: sapwood allocation fraction
variables.definition: Fraction of total growth G allocated to sapwood/wood (defined in Section S.8).

variables.name: horizontal-root allocation fraction in layer i
variables.definition: Fraction of total growth G allocated to horizontal root pool in soil layer i (defined in Section S.8).

variables.name: vertical-root allocation fraction in layer i
variables.definition: Fraction of total growth G allocated to vertical root pool in soil layer i (defined in Section S.8).

variables.name: leaf carbon pool
variables.definition: Structural leaf carbon pool updated by allocation and senescence.

variables.name: sapwood carbon pool
variables.definition: Structural sapwood carbon pool updated by allocation and senescence.

variables.name: horizontal-root carbon pool in soil layer i
variables.definition: Horizontal root pool carbon in layer i updated by allocation and senescence.

variables.name: vertical-root carbon pool in soil layer i
variables.definition: Vertical root pool carbon in layer i updated by allocation and senescence.

variables.name: leaf senescence flux
variables.definition: Leaf senescence loss flux (defined in Section S.7).

variables.name: sapwood senescence flux
variables.definition: Sapwood senescence loss flux (defined in Section S.7) and source term for heartwood growth.

variables.name: horizontal-root senescence flux in layer i
variables.definition: Senescence loss flux for horizontal root pool in layer i (defined in Section S.7).

variables.name: vertical-root senescence flux in layer i
variables.definition: Senescence loss flux for vertical root pool in layer i (defined in Section S.7).

variables.name: heartwood carbon pool
variables.definition: Heartwood carbon pool increased by sapwood senescence in THORP.

variables.name: basal diameter
variables.definition: Basal diameter updated as carbon pools evolve, via Eq. (S.9.8).

variables.name: wood carbon pool used for diameter sensitivity
variables.definition: Wood carbon state used in the derivative ∂D/∂CW (defined elsewhere).

inputs: State: C_S, C_L, C_{sW}, C_{R,H,i}, C_{R,V,i}, C_{hW}, D
Fluxes/terms: a_L(A_n+R_d), R_m, S_L, S_{sW}, S_{R,H,i}, S_{R,V,i}
Allocation fractions: u_L, u_{sW}, u_{R,H,i}, u_{R,V,i}
Allometric derivative: \partial D/\partial C_W (defined in Section S.1)
outputs: Updated pools: C_S, C_L, C_{sW}, C_{R,H,i}, C_{R,V,i}, C_{hW}
Updated geometry: D (and downstream H, W as noted)
steps: Compute growth rate G via Eq. (S.9.2a) with gT(Ta) from Eq. (S.9.2b).
Update structural pools via Eqs. (S.9.3)–(S.9.6) using allocation fractions and senescence rates (defined earlier).
Add senesced sapwood carbon to heartwood via Eq. (S.9.7).

Using updated D, compute updated H and W from power-law allometries (referenced to Section S.1); update leaf area as aL = X_L·C_L; compute stem and root maximum hydraulic conductances per Sections S.3 and S.4 (as stated in text).

evidence.pointer: All S.9 equations and concluding paragraph describing geometry/area/hydraulics updates
S.9 opening paragraph (uncoupled growth–photosynthesis; NSC definition)

limitations.text: The authors note the growth dynamics representation (linearized NSC dependence) is crude and intended for future improvement; the present focus is biomass partitioning for a given tree size rather than accurate growth rate/age dynamics.

chunk_plan.title: S.9 Carbon Pool Dynamics
chunk_plan.rationale: Defines NSC-driven growth, construction respiration fraction, structural pool ODEs, heartwood routing, and diameter update linking allocation to allometry.

chunk_plan.title: S.10 Simulation Descriptions
chunk_plan.rationale: Describes the simulation experiments, parameterization choices, boundary conditions, and site/scenario settings used to generate main-text results.
